title: Release Automation, npm Trusted Publishing, and Change Control · CI/CD Control Room
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working multi-artifact release and publication policy for release automation, npm trusted publishing, and change control. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of multi-artifact release and publication policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.
Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.
Publish with npm trusted publishing on supported hosted runners using exact repository, workflow, environment, id-token permission, and npm 11.5.1 or later.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Release Version Source Authority
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Release Version Source Authority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Release Version Source Authority, keep Ci Release Version Source Authority connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Release Approval Separation Duty
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-release-approval-separation-duty-1u5fau". Tailor its release-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Release Approval Separation Duty.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Release Approval Separation Duty into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, keep Ci Release Approval Separation Duty connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-release-approval-separation-duty-1u5fau
  - competency: ci-release-approval-separation-duty
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: release approval separation duty
    environment: multi-platform Buildx release to a non-production registry
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: job result dependency graph exact command and first causal failure
    case-key: 1u5fau
    change: |
      source: protected-reviewed-tag
      checks: required-at-source-sha
      build: once
      approval: promote-existing-artifact
      image: immutable-digest
      npm: trusted-publishing
      npm-minimum: 11.5.1
      node-minimum: 22.14.0
      id-token: release-job-only
      package-provenance: verified
      separation: author-reviewer-builder-approver-publisher
    verify: compare tag authority source checks artifact digest package contents trusted publisher identity provenance approval and published result
    failure: Reject tag-only authorization, per-environment rebuild, long-lived publish token, OIDC for private install, provenance as benign-code proof, or approval count as safety.
    repair: Protect source authority, build once, approve promotion, publish through exact OIDC identity, verify package provenance, and separate duties by risk.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Ci Release Approval Separation Duty structural check is only one gate. multi-artifact release and publication policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Release Approval Separation Duty precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: More manual approval steps always improve release safety.
